found.quote: Uptime, latency, support targets, service credits, escalation rights, and remediation plans are aspirational and do not create any remedy for Customer.

Vendor may provide as little as 5 days' notice before any model change, deprecation, replacement, or hosted-model swap that could materially affect behavior, safety, latency, or output quality. Vendor may make urgent or operational model changes without prior notice whenever it determines notice is impractical.
The cap applies to all claims and obligations, including data breaches, confidentiality breaches, security violations, payment obligations, willful misconduct, and IP indemnity claims.

Vendor has no obligation to assist with Customer's regulatory-change requests, transparency artifacts, model documentation, risk summaries, technical information, conformity-support materials, or other documentation for Customer's AI compliance obligations unless the parties sign a separate paid statement of work.
